title: SAGE Publications: Emotion Review: Table of Contents

items.title: Emotions as High-Impact Decisions: A Goal-Directed Theory
items.author: Agnes MoorsResearch Group of Quantitative Psychology and Individual Differences, Center for Social and Cultural Psychology, KU Leuven—University of Leuven, Leuven, Belgium
items.content: <article>Emotion Review, Ahead of Print. I defend the adequacy of the goal-directed theory (GDT) of emotion. After outlining the properties of emotions (mental and bodily aspects, valence, intensity, urgency, automaticity, and irrationality) and common emotion types that emotion theories seek to ...</article>

items.title: Moors’ Eliminativism Cross-Examined: Why Emotions Are Not Goal-Directed Cycles
items.author: Andrea ScarantinoDepartment of Philosophy, 1373Georgia State University, Atlanta, GA, USA
items.content: <article>Emotion Review, Ahead of Print. Moors’ eliminativist theory of emotions aims to show that understanding emotional behavior as goal-directed behavior when the goals are high value explains all that is worth explaining about behavior without invoking the concept of emotion. I argue that ...</article>
